html:
Exclusive to:

Lotus Flowers are edible Vegetable Items exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. They can be harvested from Lotus Plants found in the Lily Pond Biome. Raw or cooked, Lotus Flowers take 10 Days to spoil, and cannot be used in a Crock Pot. The Lotus Flower regrows in 5 days after it has been harvested.

Another way to obtain Lotus Flowers is through trawling in Hamlet Lily Ponds (3.92% chance).

Cooked Lotus Root

Cooked Lotus Root is an edible Vegetable Food item made by Cooking a Lotus Flower on a Campfire, Fire Pit, or other viable source of fire. It restores the same stats as its raw counterpart except for Sanity, which it restores 3 times as much as its counterpart.

Usage

×3

Tips

  • Eating Cooked Lotus Roots restores substantially more Sanity than eating the raw blossom, so cooking it is recommended.
  • Picking a Lotus Flower may spawn a Platapine, which will eat all other Lotus blossoms in the area.
  • To restore her hunger or sanity, Wickerbottom can mass-harvest Lotus Flowers using Applied Horticulture, with only minimal net decreases in sanity, if any.
  • Lotus Flowers, especially when cooked, are a great way to keep sanity at bay during the early game.

Trivia

  • This item being a source of sanity may be a reference to the lotus-eaters in Greek mythology, who in the Odyssey ate plants referred to as a lotus and fell into a calming stupor.

Gallery

  • Unused Dropped Model of Lotus Flower
  • Concept art from 2016 indicates that Lotus Flowers may had originally been planned to be included in the Shipwrecked DLC.
  • Lotus assets.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“A lovely science flower.”
–Wilson, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Just a dumb flower.”
–Willow, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Is puny pond flower.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“This beauty will also die.”
–Wendy, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“UGLY WATER FLOWER”
–WX-78, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“The blossom of Nelumbo nucifera. It's quite inspiring.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“That's a beaut.”
–Woodie, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“I prefer blooms of a darker shade.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Theoretically, it's edible. Though I should cook it to avoid stomach problems.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“It pössesses Freya's great beauty.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Gosh, this sure is beautiful.”
–Webber, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Pretty!”
–Walani, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“I should cook something up with this.”
–Warly, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Tis a flow'r'o'th'land.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“YON FLOWER O' NUM NUMS”
–Wilba, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining a Lotus Flower.
“Looks edible.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Lotus Flower.
Lotus Flowers are edible Vegetable Items exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. They can be harvested from Lotus Plants found in the Lily Pond Biome. Raw or cooked, Lotus Flowers take 10 Days to spoil, and cannot be used in a Crock Pot. The Lotus Flower regrows in 5 days after it has been harvested.

“One makes due.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.
“A salubrious plant capable of boosting mental wellness.”

“I care nöt tö cönsume this.”

“This looks delicious.”

“Mmmm...Tasty.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.
“A fine delicacy!”

“It b'cooked.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.
“FWOOSH'DED FLOWER O' NUM NUMS”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.
“Good in belly”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.
“Not the first time I've eaten flowers.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Lotus Root.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Hoa Sen là Vật Phẩm Rau củ ăn được độc quyền trong Hamlet DLC. Chúng có thể được thu hoạch từ Cây Sen tìm thấy trong Quần xã Ao Lily. Sống hoặc nấu chín, Hoa Sen mất 10 Ngày để hư hỏng và không thể sử dụng trong Nồi Hầm. Hoa sen sẽ mọc lại sau 5 ngày kể từ khi được thu hoạch.

'''Lotus Flowers''' are [[Food|edible]] [[Vegetable]] [[Item]]s exclusive to the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC. They can be harvested from [[Lotus Plant]]s found in the [[Lily Pond]] [[Biome]]. Raw or cooked, Lotus Flowers take 10 [[Day]]s to spoil, and cannot be used in a [[Crock Pot]]. The Lotus Flower regrows in 5 days after it has been harvested.

Another way to obtain Lotus Flowers is through [[Trawl Net|trawling]] in Hamlet [[Lily Pond|Lily Ponds]] (3.92% chance).
{{clear}}

It restores the same stats as its raw counterpart except for [[Sanity]], which it restores 3 times as much as its counterpart.
{{Clear}}
== {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage ==
{{Recipe
|item = Lotus Flower
|tool = Pig Collector
|result = Oinc
|resultcount = 3}}

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Eating Cooked Lotus Roots restores substantially more [[Sanity]] than eating the raw blossom, so cooking it is recommended.
* Picking a Lotus Flower may spawn a [[Platapine]], which will eat all other Lotus blossoms in the area.
* To restore her hunger or sanity, [[Wickerbottom]] can mass-harvest Lotus Flowers using [[Applied Horticulture]], with only minimal net decreases in sanity, if any.
* Lotus Flowers, especially when cooked, are a great way to keep [[sanity]] at bay during the early game.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* This item being a source of sanity may be a reference to the [[Wikipedia:Lotus-eaters|lotus-eaters]] in Greek mythology, who in the ''[[Wikipedia:Odyssey|Odyssey]]'' ate plants referred to as a lotus and fell into a calming stupor.
